Chapter XXXV—Signs of Rainbows
f^^fgcTi: ^T: i
t*5;Hf«fRT ^ 11^ U
The multi-coloured rays of the Sun, being dispersed by the
wind in a cloudy sky, are seen in the form of a bow which is
called the Rainbow.
[Here the author shows his knowledge that the Sunlight is
composed of 7 colours, which are called 7 horses figuratively. The
clouds act as a prism to disperse the sunlight into its component
colour.]
